race_meta.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

Never So Brave.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Never So Brave.spotlight: Merely mid-division in the Group 1 QEII at Ascot (1m, good) last October and again below his best on reappearance at Sandown (1m, good) in April; however, he pulled hard last time and holds leading claims if rediscovering the form of his Group 1 City Of York win (7f, good to firm) last August; mixed messages in his form regarding the suitability of slow ground.
Never So Brave.diomed: Pulled hard on comeback run at Sandown; leading claims on last August's Group 1 York win

Alcantor.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Alcantor.spotlight: Front-runner who won three Group 3s in France for Andre Fabre (1m, testing ground) and was a very close fourth in the Group 1 Prix du Moulin at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) last September; below-par fourth on stable debut at Leopardstown (1m, good; Listed) four weeks ago but may have needed the run; of interest if rediscovering his best French form.
Alcantor.diomed: Three Group 3 wins in France and may have needed the run on last month's stable debut

Ten Bob Tony.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Ten Bob Tony.spotlight: Inconsistent and he disappointed in Listed race at Haydock (7f, good) last time; however, he reportedly lost a shoe and prior to that was an encouraging fourth on his reappearance at the Curragh (7f, soft to heavy); won this race last year (good; at its old home Haydock) and posted fine third in Group 1 Foret at Longchamp (7f, very soft) last October; player if bouncing back.
Ten Bob Tony.diomed: Disappointing last time but won this race last year (at its old home Haydock)

Witness Stand.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Witness Stand.spotlight: Listed winner on soft ground who won the Group 2 Lennox Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) last July before excellent second in the Group 2 Hungerford at Newbury (7f, good to firm) in August; he's lost his form on his last four starts but perhaps wind surgery will have done the trick; wears a first-time tongue-tie and capable of having a big say if rediscovering his best.
Witness Stand.diomed: Strong Group 2 form last summer; struggled since but perhaps a wind op will do the trick

Poet Master.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Poet Master.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Poet Master.spotlight: Group 2 winner at the Curragh (7f, good) in July 2024 and runner-up in two 7f Group races last autumn (very soft/good); not at his best on his two runs this season but each-way claims if back on song today.
Poet Master.diomed: Runner-up in two Group races last autumn; below best this term; e-w claims if back on song

Golden Mind.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Golden Mind.spotlight: 5yo who won three handicaps last year (including on heavy ground) and has been better than ever in such races this season, finishing second at Musselburgh (7f, good to soft) then fourth of 28 in the Victoria Cup at Ascot (7f, good); however, he has something to find against today's opponents.
Golden Mind.diomed: Better than ever in handicaps this season but has something to find today

Chicago Critic.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Chicago Critic.spotlight: Short-headed in a Group 2 at Meydan (7f, good) in January but he needs a bigger performance if he's to take this and he hasn't been at the top of his game on his three starts since, most recently at the Curragh.
Chicago Critic.diomed: Short-headed in Meydan Group 2 in January but needs a bigger performance to take this

Balmacara.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)
Balmacara.spotlight: Listed runner-up at Sandown (1m, soft) last September and third in the same grade at Haydock (7f, good) four weeks ago on reappearance; however, he was comfortably beaten in that latest start and is 0-11 since winning novice contests at Doncaster in spring 2024 on second and third starts; up against it.
Balmacara.diomed: Has run well in defeat in Listed races but looks up against it in today's company

Shes Perfect.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Shes Perfect.spotlight: All three runs last season were in France; suffered a heartbreaking demotion for connections when placed second in the French 1,000 Guineas at Longchamp (1m, good) and failed to stay an extended 1m2f in the Diane at Chantilly last June; not seen again until third in 1m Goodwood Listed event five weeks ago, fading late on as if the run was needed; should be spot on now.
Shes Perfect.diomed: Led for long way when third in Goodwood Listed on return and should be spot on now

Sparks Fly.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Sparks Fly.spotlight: Four-time Listed winner who made Group 3 breakthrough at Saint-Cloud (1m, very soft) last October; ran well when 2l fourth in Group 2 Huxley Stakes at Chester (10.3f, good) four weeks ago; third in this last year and a penalty shouldn't prevent her being competitive again.
Sparks Fly.diomed: Third in this last year; won French Group 3 last autumn; creditable 4th at Chester latest

Pina Sonata.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Pina Sonata.spotlight: Came up well short in Group 1/Listed events in the second half of 2025 but different story on Kempton AW reappearance, readily seeing off ten rivals in 1m Listed race; winner on good to soft; capable of making her presence felt, with further progress a distinct possibility after only six starts.
Pina Sonata.diomed: AW Listed winner on reappearance and further progress is possible after only six starts

Pacific Mission.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Pacific Mission.spotlight: Runner-up in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and the Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm) last autumn; below that level when fifth in 1m York Listed race on reappearance but it's possible she simply needed the outing; player if back to her best.
Pacific Mission.diomed: Second in Group 2 and Grade 1 over 1m last year; may have needed return; player if at best

Arisaig.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Arisaig.spotlight: Smart hold-up mare who is closely matched with Shes Perfect on latest Goodwood Listed form over 1m on good; 0-6 at Listed/Group level but she's capable of a prominent showing if things fall right.
Arisaig.diomed: Smart hold-up mare who is closely matched with Shes Perfect on latest Goodwood form

Princess Child.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Princess Child.spotlight: Group 3 winner at Tipperary (7.5f, yielding) at the end of last summer; matched that form when second in 1m Group 3 at the Curragh (soft/heavy) but needs to bounce back from a lesser effort at the Curragh last time.
Princess Child.diomed: Group 3 winner last year; second at same level this year but latest effort was below par

Breckenbrough.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Breckenbrough.spotlight: French raider who won a 1m Saint-Cloud Listed race on soft last autumn; arrives on the back of creditable in-frame efforts in similar races at Longchamp (7f/1m) this spring; often races prominently.
Breckenbrough.diomed: Listed winner in France last autumn; creditable efforts in similar races there this spring

Love Dynasty.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Love Dynasty.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo who left the impression 1m2f just stretched her when fourth in Nottingham Listed race on good-ground reappearance; raced only on soft/heavy prior to that, so won't be inconvenienced however much rain arrives.
Love Dynasty.diomed: Very lightly raced 5yo who has raced only on good or softer; drop back in trip is a help

Betty Clover.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)
Betty Clover.spotlight: Won a Listed race at York (5f, good) as a 2yo but she's 0-13 since and finished behind Shes Perfect and Arisaig in a Goodwood Listed event last time.
Betty Clover.diomed: Listed win at 2 but 0-13 since; held by Shes Perfect and Arisaig on latest Goodwood form

Calandagan.spotlight: Superstar gelding who has a record of 222211111 since racing at Group 1 level and is the highest-rated racehorse in the world; couldn't quite get past Jan Brueghel in this race last year but turned the tables in the King George and is on an impressive roll going into another rematch with that rival; prevailed at Meydan (Sheema Classic) last time despite the race not panning out ideally; duly strongly respected, assuming the ground is okay (not certain to run if it's a slog scenario).
Calandagan.diomed: Second in this race last year; has since become the world's highest-rated racehorse

Jan Brueghel.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Jan Brueghel.spotlight: High-class performer who has a record of 6-8 overall and 2-3 at Group 1 level, adding to his 2024 St Leger win when knuckling down the better to beat Calandagan in this contest (good to soft) 12 months ago; combination of cheekpieces and setting an unsuitably steady pace perhaps contributed to his King George defeat behind that rival; reappearance success in the Ormonde at Chester (1m5f, good) sets him up nicely for another Coronation Cup bid.
Jan Brueghel.diomed: High-class horse who knuckled down the better to beat Calandagan in this race last year

Convergent.spotlight: Scored on soft ground on debut; flopped in the German race won by Bay City Roller but otherwise progressive and overcame trouble to defy a Group 2 penalty in Group 3 contest at Newbury (1m4f, good) on seasonal debut, taking record to 5-8; about 3l behind Lambourn at Chester last term but is now in the same ball park as that opponent on ratings, with a chance of further improvement to boot; adds great interest to this race.
Convergent.diomed: Overcame trouble to win at Newbury on reappearance; progressive and adds great interest

Lambourn.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Lambourn.spotlight: Most wins on good ground but has form on soft; held on by only a neck from Bay City Roller on better terms in the Huxley (1m2f Group 2) at Chester on seasonal debut but proved game in first-time cheekpieces and, based on Ryan Moore's post-race comments, will come on for the run; notable contender in the retained headgear returned to the scene of his biggest moment, namely a clearcut success in last year's Derby (followed up in the Irish version).
Lambourn.diomed: Notable contender returned to the scene of his biggest moment, namely 2025 Derby win

Bay City Roller.spotlight: Consistent colt whose Group 1 form is non-domestic, namely a success in Germany (1m4f, soft) last November and good second at the Curragh (10.5f, good) latest start; only a neck behind Lambourn on worse terms at Chester on reappearance, albeit with that rival rusty and likely to improve for the run, according to Ryan Moore; needs a personal best to land this prize.
Bay City Roller.diomed: Consistent but his Group 1 form is non-domestic; needs a personal best to win this race

Illinois.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Illinois.spotlight: Runner-up in two Cup races last term; only a neck behind stablemate Jan Brueghel in the 2024 St Leger but was beaten about 5l by that opponent last month when bidding for a second success in the Ormonde at Chester, doing nothing to close their current ratings differential; perhaps in here to set the pace and ensure a proper test.
Illinois.diomed: One of three Ballydoyle runners; perhaps in here to set the pace and ensure a proper test

Kinswoman.spotlight: Progressive when dropped to 5f in the second half of 2025, winning a Windsor novice (good to firm) and adding handicap wins at Chester (good to firm) and Haydock (good); unable to justify odds-on favouritism at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) 24 days ago on her return from seven months off but it was still a solid effort and she remains capable of better; another 3lb higher today but sure to be popular.
Kinswoman.diomed: Progressive in 2025 and reappearance was encouraging; up in weights but better to come

Lexington Blitz.spotlight: Picked off late in the 3yo Dash 12 months ago; gelded prior to his seasonal return and he has run well on both starts in 2026, following his close third at Musselburgh with an impressive win at Goodwood five weeks ago (5f, good); a 9lb rise asks a stiffer question but he is progressive.
Lexington Blitz.diomed: Second at this meeting last year; impressed at Goodwood latest; up 9lb but on the upgrade

Dyonisos.spotlight: Yet to win for Ian Williams but he's down in the weights and has run well in two of his last three starts, pipped at Goodwood last month (5f, good); wouldn't be sure to confirm those placings with Dream Composer this time.
Dyonisos.diomed: Second in two of his last three; not sure to confirm latest placing with Dream Composer

Eclairage.spotlight: All four wins have come on the AW at Dundalk and she wasn't beaten far in a Group 3 there on her final run in 2025; fully effective on turf and ran a cracker at Navan three weeks ago, showing her trademark early speed before being picked off late on (front two pulled 6l clear); 8lb higher today but this track should play to her strengths; chance.
Eclairage.diomed: Speedy front-runner; fine second at Navan 3 weeks ago; 8lb higher but foolish to discount

Democracy Dilemma.spotlight: Front-runner; second in this race in 2024 off 3lb higher; ran away with a 5f handicap at Haydock (good ground) last September off this mark; unable to hit the same heights since but his AW third in February was a fair run; won't be inconvenienced should there be a marked deterioration in the going but a full revival is required; visor returns having been left off latest.
Democracy Dilemma.diomed: Second here in 2024 off 3lb higher; has not found very best in 2026; needs a full revival

Arklow Lad.spotlight: 2-4 since joining Mick Appleby, notably a Class 2 handicap win at Pontefract (5f, good to soft; cheekpieces first time) in April; solid run at York three weeks ago and one for whom rain would be no concern.
Arklow Lad.diomed: 2-4 for Mick Appleby; solid effort at York last month; one to consider

Dream Composer.spotlight: Trained by James Evans when winning this race in 2024 off a 5lb higher mark (good to soft); well beaten in it last year but he arrives in good form for his new stable, winning at Ripon in April and not enjoying the cleanest run when fourth at Goodwood 15 days ago; rain won't hurt his chance and he's of obvious interest.
Dream Composer.diomed: Two good runs since joining Tony Carroll; won this race in 2024 off 5lb higher; big player

Stormy Impact.spotlight: In 2025 she took a big step forward from her seasonal return when sweeping through late to collar Lexington Blitz in the 3yo Dash on this card; also won at Wolverhampton in July (6f, AW) off 1lb higher than today; never a threat at York last month on return to action but it's not unreasonable to expect much better today and she is sure to get a strong pace to aim at.
Stormy Impact.diomed: Fast finish to peg back Lexington Blitz in 3yo Dash in 2025; can leave reappearance behind

King Of Light.spotlight: Won a 5f handicap (good to soft; first time in tongue-tie/cheekpieces combination) at Haydock last September off a 6lb lower mark; stayed on for second behind the impressive Lexington Blitz at Goodwood on last month's return; withdrawn at York two weeks ago having got upset in the stalls (9-1 at the time of withdrawal); the more rain the better; no accessories today.
King Of Light.diomed: Encouraging seasonal return at Goodwood; sharper today and the more rain the better

Brazen Bolt.spotlight: Won big-field 5f handicaps at York and Goodwood last July; two solid efforts in defeat this season and he won on his only visit to Epsom, albeit over 7f; not impossibly treated but he wouldn't want the ground to be any slower than good.
Brazen Bolt.diomed: Two solid efforts this year and 1-1 at Epsom; suited by good/firmer

Vintage Clarets.spotlight: Prolific winner down the years and he arrives off an 11lb lower mark than when fifth in this 12 months ago; yet to shine this year (poor draw at Chester on penultimate start) but it wouldn't be a huge shock to see him fare better today.
Vintage Clarets.diomed: Prolific winner down the years; on a dangerous mark and revival isn't out of the question

Emperor Spirit.spotlight: Appreciated the return to turf when finishing second of 17 at Ascot five weeks ago; 1lb rise still leaves him on a feasible mark; rarely seen on slower than good.
Emperor Spirit.diomed: Good 2nd at Ascot on last month's return to turf; rain would be a concern

Rhythm N Hooves.spotlight: Admirable campaigner who is gradually edging down the weights; type to pop up soon but perhaps in a slightly less competitive affair.
Rhythm N Hooves.diomed: Handicapped to win soon but this race might prove a shade too competitive

Almaty Star.spotlight: Better on AW than turf but took advantage of a good opportunity at Yarmouth in April (5f, good); hit with an 8lb rise for that, bringing his turf and AW marks into line; blew the start back at Yarmouth last month but the stewards deemed him a non-runner due to having his head turned as the stalls opened; risky on the back of that and there are other pace influences to contend with today.
Almaty Star.diomed: Better on AW; in a much deeper race than the one he won at Yarmouth eight weeks ago

Betsen.spotlight: 6f AW win on his stable debut in November; added to his tally when seeing off five rivals at Chepstow 16 days ago (Class 4; 5f, good to soft); in deeper today.
Betsen.diomed: Drop back to 5f saw him win a Class 4 at Chepstow last month; in much deeper today

Cindy Lou Who.spotlight: Progressive in 2025 and she took her form to a new level when second of 14 in a Bath Listed event on her reappearance (5f, good); not in the same form at Newbury (6f, good) when last seen seven weeks ago and she is now 11lb higher; others look stronger.
Cindy Lou Who.diomed: Bath 2nd on return was a fine effort but disappointing last time and now 11lb higher

Ziggy's Triton.spotlight: Saw off 11 rivals when scoring at Musselburgh in April (5f, good; Class 4) but he has had his limitations exposed in stronger company twice since; beaten only 3l at York two weeks ago but others are more appealing in today's field.
Ziggy's Triton.diomed: Musselburgh win in April came in Class 4; held in stronger company twice since

Another Baar.spotlight: Acts on any ground; having dropped in the weights he enjoyed a revival last August/September, rattling off a four-timer (5f-5.3f); had a stint in Bahrain over the winter, unplaced on four occasions but recording a career best when winning a 5f event (good to firm) in January; looks on a tough mark back in Britain.
Another Baar.diomed: Reeled off a four-timer last term; 5f win in Bahrain in January; looks high in weights

Rogue Enforcer.spotlight: Suited by good ground or slower; won a 5f Listed race in Italy on his final start for a French yard; big prices and finished down the field in two 5f handicaps last month; dropped 7lb for his troubles but he arrives with a point to prove.
Rogue Enforcer.diomed: Listed win in Italy last October; well held at big prices in two starts for his new stable

Star Chorus.spotlight: In good form on AW for Mike Murphy this winter, winning twice at Southwell (5f/6f); slowly away (blindfold removed late) at Goodwood two weeks ago; looks vulnerable at this level and goes without accessories this time.
Star Chorus.diomed: Two AW wins this winter; slowly away at Goodwood latest; vulnerable at this level

Benvenuto Cellini.spotlight: Frankel colt who was dominant in a maiden last July, a Group 2 at Leopardstown (1m) in September and Group 3 Chester Vase (12.5f, good) one month ago; his free-flowing, long-striding action was seen to good effect in those races, but not on heavy ground when third in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m) in October; soft ground would also introduce something for him to prove but otherwise he brings strength of stamina and among the best form; if the ground is okay, a bold show looks assured.
Benvenuto Cellini.diomed: Stays well, good form; something to prove on soft/heavy, otherwise bold show looks assured

Item.spotlight: Well on top last September in a maiden at Kempton (7f, AW) and novice at Bath (1m, good to firm); non-runner in Group 1 Futurity due to heavy going; not the easiest to settle when reappearing in Group 2 Dante at York (10.2f, good; 11-2) last month but he mastered front-running Action in the last half furlong to win it well, putting Christmas Day in the shade as well; settling a bit better (fine as 2yo) would cement claims for 1m4f but this son of Frankel won going away and brings clear potential, plus leading form and an unbeaten record.
Item.diomed: Settling a bit better would help but he won going away in Group 2 Dante at York (10.2f)

Pierre Bonnard.spotlight: Asserted late on to finish firmly on top last October in a Group 3 at Newmarket (good to firm) and Group 1 Criterium de Saint-Cloud (very soft) and they were over 1m2f; 3yo campaign got off to a muted start but he briefly hit the front in Derby Trial at Leopardstown before James J Braddock swooped and Aidan O'Brien has stressed that he went gently with him at home until after that race; his sire and dam's sire were Derby winners (dam won 1m2f Group 1 Nassau) and he now moves up from 1m2f; he looked every inch a serious Derby contender last term and that's probably still the impression to go with.
Pierre Bonnard.diomed: Looked every inch a serious Derby contender as 2yo and that impression still holds sway

James J Braddock.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake
James J Braddock.spotlight: Second start as 2yo was a 6l win in Curragh maiden (1m, heavy); never able to challenge on return in the Group 3 Ballysax won by Christmas Day and couldn't have left it any later in the Derby Trial back over Leopardstown's 1m2f (good) four weeks ago, but upstaging Pierre Bonnard so dramatically in the latter reinforces the feeling that 1m4f will suit him well; his sire's sole Group 1 win was at this trip; each-way player for trainer and jockey who won the Oaks yesterday.
James J Braddock.diomed: Swooped on Pierre Bonnard in the 1m2f Leopardstown Derby Trial and 1m4f will suit him well

Maltese Cross.spotlight: Debut was on soft; all four of his races were tight finishes and he won the last three; pulled it out of the fire against useful rivals in a valuable novice event at Newbury (1m2f, good) and narrowly maintained an advantage over Bay Of Brilliance as the pair drew clear in a ding-dong battle for the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm) four weeks ago; his pedigree is all about 1m4f, including with his sire and dam's sire both Derby winners, and he also shapes as if every yard today will suit; needs to pull out more again but has to be in each-way calculations.
Maltese Cross.diomed: Won his last three; pedigree and performance are all about 1m4f; firmly in the e-w picture

Action.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Action.spotlight: Closely related to 2025 Derby winner Lambourn; carries his head high but ended last season with a close, battling second in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy) and front-running second in the Dante at York (10.2f, good) last month was of a similar order, albeit no match for Item in the final 1f; a disappointing, sluggish display on reappearance was his only start with tongue tied; likely to stay 1m4f, with a possible place role but it's unlikely to be more unless the new cheekpieces have a major positive effect.
Action.diomed: 2nd in 2yo Group 1 (heavy) and to Item in Dante; carries head high and cheekpieces go on

Bay Of Brilliance.spotlight: Half-brother to smart Flat and hurdle winner Absurde; odds-on winner of a maiden at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) and novice at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft; made all) on last two starts as 2yo; pushed Maltese Cross all the way in Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm) and could improve on that first-time-out display; more to do but he's heading the right way.
Bay Of Brilliance.diomed: Pushed Maltese Cross all the way in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f) on reappearance

Ancient Egypt.spotlight: Frankel colt bought for 1,100,000gns as a yearling; all four races on good to firm; Beverley and Goodwood novice wins last summer before the Group 2 Royal Lodge saw a comprehensive end to his unbeaten record; he returned to Newmarket, however, with an ultimately authoritative win in a five-runner Listed race (1m2f) five weeks ago, helping to make the running; 1m4f beckons strongly after that and he's included in each-way calculations.
Ancient Egypt.diomed: 1m4f beckons strongly after Newmarket Listed win, putting him in each-way calculations

Christmas Day.spotlight: Maiden success was on heavy going; Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding) on April reappearance made it three wins in a row and his Dante third at York (10.2f, good) next time was at least as good on form; while lacking anything like the same dash as winner Item at York, and also unable to get to grips with stablemate Action, he did plug on as if 1m4f could help; it clearly needs to.
Christmas Day.diomed: Put in his place by Item in the Dante at York, albeit plugging on as if 1m4f could help

Alderman.spotlight: Travelled well and challenged from the rear at Newbury (1m3f, good; 17-2) on reappearance seven weeks ago, sticking to his task manfully too while chasing home an exciting prospect at a respectful distance; a promising, scopey sort from a famous middle-distance family but his progressive form in maidens, without a win, does not make him a serious Derby contender.
Alderman.diomed: Promising, scopey sort from famous middle-distance family but he's been running in maidens

Balzac.spotlight: Novice winner whose rallying third of six in Listed race here (1m2f, good) in April suggested that longer trips would suit; tried 11.6f in the Lingfield Derby Trial (good to firm) and recorded his highest rating, but Maltese Cross and Bay Of Brilliance were just over 6l ahead of him; major work to do.
Balzac.diomed: Lingfield Derby Trial 3rd but Maltese Cross and Bay Of Brilliance were just over 6l ahead

Rebel Rocker.spotlight: Strong late on to grab a maiden at Kempton (1m, AW) in November and came from last to second in the six-runner Listed Trial here (1m2f, good; 33-1) in April; hopes for the latter form seem to have been punctured and although he looks capable of better and shapes as if 1m4f will suit him well, 1m4f in something other than the Derby would seem much more realistic.
Rebel Rocker.diomed: 2nd in the Listed Trial here; shapes as if 1m4f will suit him well but faces a stiff task

A Taste Of Glory.spotlight: Won a Brighton maiden and 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield; however, he ended last season with last of ten in a French Group 1 (1m2f, very soft) and subsided rapidly to be last of six in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm; 33-1); his owner is renowned for Derby contenders who have run well at big prices, but hopes for this one may be stretching it.
A Taste Of Glory.diomed: 33-1, subsided rapidly to be last of six in Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm)

Poker.spotlight: Having fetched 4,300,000gns in 2024, he is the most expensive yearling colt ever sold at auction in Europe; by Wootton Bassett out of a sister to Oaks winner Was; on the other hand, he was left nearly 9l behind by Bay Of Brilliance at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft) in October and sported cheekpieces at 10-1 when a front-running second of four to a stablemate in Haydock novice (1m4f, good to firm) in April; admirably tenacious in the latter but the dream was once of so much more.
Poker.diomed: Front-running second of four in a 1m4f Haydock novice in April, so has a mountain to climb

Hell Yeah He Did.spotlight: All three turf races as 2yo were on soft or heavy, making most and rallying stoutly to win a maiden at Nottingham (1m) on final outing; gelded since; seemingly no doubt that he will still be suited by 1m2f+ and nothing to prove if the ground is testing; one to note on seasonal/handicap debut.
Hell Yeah He Did.diomed: Will be suited by 1m2f+ and nothing to prove if ground is testing; seasonal/handicap debut

Allegresse.spotlight: Won from the front in two Kempton novices (1m, AW); weakened out of it in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f) on final 2yo run and mastered by his three rivals late on in a conditions stakes at Goodwood (1m, good; unraced on softer) on reappearance, so he does not present the most glittering case for this handicap debut, but stamina in family suggests he should stay.
Allegresse.diomed: Limitations on last two starts; handicap debut; stamina in family suggests he should stay

Starlight Time.spotlight: Won a small-field maiden here (7f, good) last July on debut; well held in eighth of 26 in a valuable sales race at Newmarket (7f, good) but he was on his own through the final 1f when making most to win at 1-2 in a novice at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in November; 1m2f is plausible and he should bring potential to this seasonal and handicap debut.
Starlight Time.diomed: 2-3 as 2yo; 1m2f plausible and he should bring potential to this seasonal/handicap debut

Folk Pageant.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Folk Pageant.spotlight: Second on good to soft on her only 2yo start; last of nine on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) in April but won her other three starts this season, in 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket and Chester on last two occasions; 6lb rise is a sterner reaction since latest start but she's seen those last two races out strongly and could have more to give; major player.
Folk Pageant.diomed: Saw it out well when winning 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket and Chester on last two starts

Lake Como.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; found serious improvement over 7f/1m on AW but, although running respectably, he's not transferred the best of that form to 1m turf handicaps on last two outings; worth a go at this new trip.
Lake Como.diomed: Has not transferred best AW form to two 1m turf handicaps; worth a go at this new trip

Pendella.spotlight: Shaped like a non-stayer over 1m4f at York on turf debut in May, but reeled in only late in the day when second of ten there over 1m2f (again good ground; raised 2lb today) nine days later; ridden by Jack Nicholls last time and that performance puts her on the shortlist.
Pendella.diomed: Reeled in late in the day when second of ten at York (1m2f, good) last time; raised 2lb

Savvy Disko.spotlight: Minor honours at Newmarket (7f, good) in October on first two starts; 7-1 for 12-runner novice at Lingfield (1m, AW; York Tower third) last month and always close up to win by a neck; getting up in the final strides there boosts hope for 1m2f (despite pedigree) and he's a promising newcomer to handicaps.
Savvy Disko.diomed: Won Lingfield novice (1m, AW) last month, always close up and leading in final strides

Silver State.spotlight: Usually ridden by Rose Dawes; won four of seven handicaps, all once visored, including from the front over 1m at Bath (good to soft) and Doncaster when returned to turf; helped to set a strong gallop when fifth of ten at Haydock latest but he needs to resume improvement and this new trip is not sure to help.
Silver State.diomed: Needs to resume improvement after helping to set strong pace in bid for 1m turf hat-trick

York Tower.spotlight: Second over 7f at Yarmouth (good) and Chelmsford (AW) on his only two starts as 2yo; respectable third to Savvy Disko in novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) on return last month; handicap newcomer, with plenty to suggest that 1m2f+ will suit and best days are ahead of him.
York Tower.diomed: Handicap newcomer; plenty suggests that 1m2f+ will suit and best days are ahead of him

Tailgunner Joe.spotlight: Better in Meydan campaign in December-March, including a 1m win and he shaped as if he stayed 1m1f on final start there; needs to rebound from a poor show at Newmarket (1m2f) in April and he was gelded the next day.
Tailgunner Joe.diomed: Needs to rebound from a poor show (1m2f) in April and he was gelded the following day

Be The Standard.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; flopped when favourite for five-runner nursery at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October and weakened into fifth of eight at Chester (1m4f; 5-1) on return, so his two handicap performances left plenty to be desired; back in trip today and perhaps he needed that latest run.
Be The Standard.diomed: Both handicap performances left plenty to be desired; back in trip for this second 3yo run

Spinning Wheel.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spinning Wheel.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spinning Wheel.spotlight: Unraced as a juvenile but now boasts 3-6 record, winning his first two and back on the scoresheet when making all at Leicester (1m4f, good to soft) in October; bumped into a quite well-regarded sort when second at Goodwood (1m6f, good) a fortnight ago and this unexposed 4yo looks a big contender to build on that encouraging return.
Spinning Wheel.diomed: Boasts 3-6 record; unexposed and looks a big contender to build on his encouraging return

Elsass.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Elsass.spotlight: Previously susceptible to the odd stinker but he's 2-2 since being gelded, scoring at Southwell (AW) in April and Hamilton (again 1m3f, good) three weeks ago; 8lb rise in a much tougher contest demands more but maybe he's putting it all together now.
Elsass.diomed: 2-2 since being gelded; 8lb rise demands more but maybe he's putting it all together now

Give It To Me Oj.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Give It To Me Oj.spotlight: Acts on soft; can make the running; won six (four over hurdles, two on Flat) of seven starts in a golden spell last February-October, including over C&D in September in both the Jump Jockeys Derby (soft) and Apprentices' Derby (good); recent run over 2m at Goodwood should have teed him up nicely for what could be a plan from last year's winning yard; considered.
Give It To Me Oj.diomed: Dual C&D winner; recent run over 2m should have teed him up nicely for this; considered

Regal Ulixes.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Regal Ulixes.spotlight: Won on soft (1m) as a 2yo; gelded after just two starts last year (included 1m2f win) and he scored off 5lb lower on AW in February; various excuses in three subsequent outings when he frequently looked as though this step back up in trip is well worth another go; respected.
Regal Ulixes.diomed: Various excuses last three starts and looks like this trip is worth a go again; respected

Night Breeze.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Night Breeze.spotlight: Finished fifth in this last year and it was just his second visit to Epsom when impressively landing the five-runner Great Metropolitan over C&D (good) in April; 4lb rise (arguably could have been worse) pushes him into uncharted territory but he has won on good to soft and is a player under Billy Loughnane.
Night Breeze.diomed: Impressed over C&D (good) in April; 4lb rise arguably could have been worse; player

Too Soon.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Too Soon.spotlight: Housed in a stable that rarely misidentifies its geese as swans, so the fact he's been highly tried on occasions is interesting; 1-14 record is therefore frustrating but the win did come here as a juvenile (8.5f, heavy) and he gets first-time cheekpieces today; suspicion he's capable of a big run with Warren Fentiman an unusual booking for the yard.
Too Soon.diomed: Sole win came here (8.5f, heavy); cheekpieces go on and he may be capable of a big run

Hengest.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Hengest.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes
Hengest.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has swiftly found his feet for this yard; built on a big run in novice here (8.5f, good) in April by going unbeaten in his three handicaps (1m2f and 11.5f, good/good to firm), penalised on the last two occasions; now bumped up markedly in grade (2lb wrong) and softer ground would pose a query but there's no telling where this improvement will end up.
Hengest.diomed: Lightly raced and unbeaten in three handicaps; ground a possible query but he's on a roll

Asgard's Captain.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Asgard's Captain.spotlight: Track regular who finished third in this race (good to soft) in 2024 and stayed on from the rear to be fourth in last year's edition; also won last season's Great Metropolitan over C&D (good) and got back in the winning groove when taking Newmarket handicap three weeks ago; latter success puts him on a career-high mark but he's unlikely to be far away.
Asgard's Captain.diomed: Goes well here and won at Newmarket latest; career-high mark but unlikely to be far away

Bulletin.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Bulletin.spotlight: Extraordinarily consistent sort who is yet to finish out of the frame in 15 starts and his profile isn't short of improvement either; stays this trip, acts on good to soft (unraced on soft) and he also ran well on his only visit here; very likeable and should go well again.
Bulletin.diomed: Not out of the frame in 15 starts; acts on good to soft and ran well on sole visit here

Dancing In Paris.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Dancing In Paris.spotlight: He hasn't won since August 2024 but ran some fine races in defeat last season, including when second of 14 at Glorious Goodwood (1m6f, good to soft) in August; those efforts have ensured his mark is holding up, however, and he makes his comeback over a trip probably short of his best these days.
Dancing In Paris.diomed: No win last term but ran some fine races; returns over a trip probably short of his best

Gordon Grey.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Gordon Grey.spotlight: The second runner for local trainer George Baker and this one has an enviable 3-4 record here, having picked up two Ladies' Derbys (2024 and 2025) in addition to the Amateurs' Derby last year when trained by Ben Brookhouse; not discounted on that score but this is a good bit hotter than those events; refitted tongue-tie could be a plus.
Gordon Grey.diomed: Won three "Derbys" over C&D; not discounted on that score but this is a good bit hotter

Antrim.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Antrim.spotlight: Often a front-runner; 3-7 on AW but he's 0-4 on turf and latest third to Elsass at Hamilton (1m3f, good) was no more than respectable; untested on good to soft or worse but his pedigree may given some encouragement, as his dam is closely related to outstanding performer Enable whose 15 wins included the 2017 Oaks over C&D and four of her Group 1 wins were on softer than good.
Antrim.diomed: 0-4 on turf and latest third (1m3f, good) was just respectable; softer ground a query

Prydwen.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Prydwen.spotlight: Prolific 8yo who gained his 11th success when taking 1m4f Ripon handicap (good) in April; got a 2lb nudge after that and received a further 3lb rise after finishing fifth in 2m Group 2 in Germany four weeks ago; while that's not ideal, he's won off higher marks and won't mind how the ground is riding.
Prydwen.diomed: Edging up the weights again but has won off higher and won't mind how the ground is riding

Lord Melbourne.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Lord Melbourne.spotlight: Won 23-runner November Handicap (1m4f, soft) in 2024 and he's 2-3 here (1m2f and 1m4f) including a win on good to soft last September; those are the clear positives but he's been poor in three starts at 1m2f (snow/AW/good) this year; has a tempting mark if he can stage a revival, so needs a market check.
Lord Melbourne.diomed: 2-3 here but poor this year; tempting mark if he can stage a revival; market check needed

Max Mayhem.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Max Mayhem.spotlight: Locally trained runner who offered something to work on when seventh of 13 at Kempton (1m4f, AW) on his British return ten days ago; also has form on good to soft but latest effort still leaves him with work to do and he finished tailed off in the 2023 running of this.
Max Mayhem.diomed: Not disgraced latest but that still leaves work to do; tailed off in the 2023 renewal

Military Academy.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Military Academy.spotlight: 1m4f AW Listed winner (also acts on good to soft and softer) for the Gosdens but he left that yard under a cloud in the autumn; rediscovered some form at 1m2f on AW this winter but he's been below par on his last three starts; soft ground would enhance his cause but others are more solid.
Military Academy.diomed: Below par on last three starts; soft ground would enhance his cause, but others more solid

Northwest Passage.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Northwest Passage.spotlight: Heavy-ground romp at Salisbury (had been gelded) in September and second at Newbury (also 1m2f, soft) the following month give him a shot; however, he left Roger Teal after two runs over hurdles and will need to improve upon his two starts for new yard this spring; 5lb out of the handicap.
Northwest Passage.diomed: Will need to improve upon his two starts for new yard this spring; 5lb out of the handicap

Fine Interview.spotlight: Well suited by soft/good to soft ground; has a record of 3-4 since wearing cheekpieces, most recently winning at Doncaster (6f) ten weeks ago; respected in the retained headgear, with further progress possible.
Fine Interview.diomed: Record of 3-4 since wearing cheekpieces; respected with further progress possible

Gold Star Hero.spotlight: Record of 4-5 on AW features a hat-trick since being gelded; chance depends on whether he can transfer the form back to turf; only 1-11 in this sphere and he is 16lb higher than last attempt.
Gold Star Hero.diomed: Record of 4-5 on AW features a hat-trick since being gelded; only 1-11 on turf

Apollo One.spotlight: Acts on any ground; ran okay behind younger rivals at Newmarket last time; losing run is mounting up but he has been placed in this race twice, finishing second in 2023 and third in 2024; again has each-way hopes.
Apollo One.diomed: Placed twice in this contest, finishing second in 2023 and third in 2024

Sondad.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; broadly consistent form last term featured a success (off 7lb lower) in this contest; likely needed his York reappearance; interesting back here with that outing under his belt.
Sondad.diomed: Won this race last year; interesting back here with York reappearance under his belt

Topwarrior.spotlight: Record of 2-9 in his first season last year, winning in blinkers at Newbury (6f, soft) final outing; may improve further this term; a possible danger, provided the removal of headgear doesn't backfire.
Topwarrior.diomed: May improve further, provided the removal of blinkers doesn't backfire; reappearance

Badri.spotlight: The oldest contender in this field but he's still quite useful and prevailed at Redcar (6f) most recently; record in this race includes a success in 2023 (off 6lb higher) and 1l third in 2025; may remain competitive.
Badri.diomed: Record in this contest includes a success in 2023 and 1l third in 2025

Partisan Hero.spotlight: Unproven on soft/heavy ground; has got faster over time and prevailed narrowly at York (5f, good) most recently, registering a first sprint win; peaking at the right time and record of 2-2 at Epsom (7f) includes a success at this meeting 12 months ago; major player.
Partisan Hero.diomed: Peaking at the right time; record of 2-2 at Epsom includes a win at this meeting last year

Saint Lawrence.spotlight: Proved resurgent with close second at Ascot (6f, good) on stable/seasonal debut; defied a stone higher mark in the Wokingham three years ago and has possibilities even though he hasn't won since.
Saint Lawrence.diomed: Resurgent at Ascot on stable/seasonal debut; won the 2023 Wokingham off a stone higher

Twilight Jet.spotlight: Won by 4l last Saturday to complete a Lingfield turf double; raised a whopping 12lb but is still well treated on best form, which includes close second (off 4lb higher) in this contest in 2025; fighting chance.
Twilight Jet.diomed: Close second in this race in 2025; has won last two starts; fighting chance

Invictus Gold.spotlight: Useful sort whose latest effort is best forgiven (chance compromised by early trouble); however, he has something to prove away from Newmarket, where most of his turf form lies.
Invictus Gold.diomed: Useful sort but has something to prove away from Newmarket

Strike Red.spotlight: Has lost his form a bit in two starts since an April success and is far from certain to raise his game much, having been beaten in this race for the last three years (finishing positions 756).
Strike Red.diomed: Beaten in this race for last three years (form figures 756)

Purest Time.spotlight: Prevailed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) most recently, proving suited by the return to turf from AW and belatedly registering a first win since joining Ian Williams, who has landed this prize twice in the last decade; one for the shortlist kept to this sphere.
Purest Time.diomed: Suited by the return to turf last time, winning at Windsor; one for the shortlist

Kylian.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell
Kylian.spotlight: Form has dipped in last two starts, taking record for current yard to 0-5; doesn't look as if he's about to prevail in this warm affair.
Kylian.diomed: 0-5 for current yard; doesn't look as if he's about to prevail in this warm race

Roman Dragon.spotlight: Has gained all eight domestic wins at Chester, the most recent on penultimate start; Epsom is an unknown and he's on a difficult mark, conceding weight all round to boot.
Roman Dragon.diomed: Has gained all domestic wins at Chester; Epsom is an unknown and mark is difficult

Eye Of Dubai.spotlight: Has the ability to take a serious hand but he ran well below market expectations in this race last year and another reappearance duck egg doesn't augur well; has shown peak form in autumn months.
Eye Of Dubai.diomed: Ran below market expectations in this race last year; best form in autumn months

Solar Aclaim.spotlight: Two duck eggs on good ground this term; ideally needs soft/heavy, so chance depends on the weather; favourably handicapped off 4lb below last winning mark.
Solar Aclaim.diomed: Ideally needs soft/heavy; favourably treated off 4lb below last winning mark
